notes: Default gravity deck for Nicola's riverboat. Warm beats (a bird lands, the kettle finds itself) cut with pressure beats (the river noses higher, somebody on the bank shouts). Endgame cards ride at the bottom and carry the heavier moments of the meal.

deck.title: the kettle finds itself
deck.flavor: the kettle has come to a boil without you. somebody is going to want tea in three minutes. somebody always does.

deck.title: a bird lands on the upper deck
deck.flavor: a small grey bird that does not belong to this season. it perches on the rosebush. it watches the meal through the upper-deck hatch.

deck.title: a call from the bank
deck.flavor: someone shouting your name from the dock. unhurried, friendly. nicola's name carries because the bank carries it the way banks carry sound across water.

deck.title: the neighbor swings by
deck.flavor: the neighbor steps onto the gangway, brings something in a covered dish. they do not bring it inside. they leave it on the boarding step and walk back to their boat.

deck.title: the river takes a breath
deck.flavor: the boat lifts an inch, lowers an inch. the kettle steadies. somebody at the long table glances at their water glass and doesn't say anything.

deck.title: the tomato vine yields
deck.flavor: two new fruits, ready, where you left one. the upper deck is generous in a way the season shouldn't have allowed.

deck.title: a guest is late
deck.flavor: the lounge has the shape of a person who is not in it yet. the cushion is uncreased. you keep looking at it. you keep not looking at it.

deck.title: a pot boils dry
deck.flavor: you weren't watching the pot. the bottom is dark. you smell it before you see it. the kitchen will smell like it for a week.

deck.title: the river widow remembers
deck.flavor: she has paused over the soup. she is remembering something. you do not interrupt. nobody at the table does. the silence is everyone's.

deck.title: frasier walks past on the bank
deck.flavor: he doesn't wave. he is carrying something rectangular — a piece of balsa, maybe, or a board for whatever model is on the workbench this week. he is looking at the boat the way he looks at finished pieces.

deck.title: the garden in full sun
deck.flavor: the upper deck is briefly the brightest thing on the river. the herbs throw shadows two ways at once because of the reflection.

deck.title: the table settles
deck.flavor: the long table is suddenly the warmest thing in the boat. somebody at it is laughing. somebody else is laughing too. somebody who hadn't been is now.

deck.title: a storm upriver
deck.flavor: the radio in the wheelhouse has come on by itself. weather. the storm is six hours away and headed the way storms head when they're going to find the bank.

deck.title: the river noses higher
deck.flavor: the hull is heavier in the water than it was an hour ago. the gangway has shifted by a finger's width. only nicola notices. only nicola has the years of noticing it.

deck.title: the meal finds itself
deck.flavor: everyone has stopped talking. they are eating. that's the moment the dinner becomes the dinner. nobody will be able to describe it tomorrow morning. they will all remember it the same way.
